source_pr.title: mocktikv: avoid sorting multiple times with unstable order
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/15767

Problem Summary:
The all tuples in TopN doesn't to be sorted multiple times but resulting into unstable order, so Next() does not fetch all tuples in the same order.

### What is changed and how it works?
after all tuples from its child are fetched, sort them immedrately. Then fetch data from the ordered data. 

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

What's Changed:

How it Works:

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

symptoms: PR title: unstable order; avoid sorting multiple times in mocktikv TopN executor.
failure_signature: PR title: unstable order; avoid sorting multiple times in mocktikv TopN executor.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: mocktikv: avoid sorting multiple times with unstable order.
fix_pattern: mocktikv: avoid sorting multiple times with unstable order

evidence: evidence: PR title: unstable order; avoid sorting multiple times in mocktikv TopN executor.

evidence: move sort.Sort(&e.heap.topNSorter) to only run once after exhausting input to avoid unstable intermediate ordering (store/mockstore/mocktikv/executor.go); adds TestIssue15767 (executor/executor_test.go).
